item.title: Apalachee High School shooting suspect pleads guilty to all charges
item.description: Colt Gray was charged with 55 counts, including malice murder, and faces up to 180 years in prison for the deadly mass shooting at Apalachee High School.

item.title: FDA reports new cyclospora outbreak as North Carolina eyes cilantro, parsley
item.description: Federal health officials said they've identified a new outbreak of the cyclospora parasite, but the source hasn't been specified.

item.title: Mentalist Oz Pearlman on why he's returning to correspondents' dinner after shooting
item.description: Mentalist Oz Pearlman, the headline entertainer at the rescheduled White House Correspondents' Dinner, discusses why he wanted to return after a gunman opened fire outside the initial event in April. Pearlman said he was scared for his life and felt "pure fear and panic" when the shooting happened, but added he feels "very secure" ahead of Friday's dinner.

item.title: Woman who works 2 jobs faces food insecurity amid price hikes: "You feel like a failure"
item.description: Over the past five years, grocery prices have jumped 32%, making food affordability a top concern for many Americans. Ash-har Quraishi shows how it's impacting families.

item.title: Exclusive discounts from CBS Mornings Deals
item.description: On this edition of CBS Mornings Deals, we show you items that will help improve your everyday lifestyle. Visit cbsdeals.com to take advantage of these exclusive deals today. CBS earns commissions on purchases made through cbsdeals.com.

item.title: Iran keeps targeting U.S. bases as U.S. interceptor stockpiles dwindle
item.description: Explosions were heard near a U.S. base in Iraq, and Iran said it targeted Bahrain, Jordan and Kuwait as the 21-week war intensifies.

item.title: What to know about the Abraham Accords, the agreement Trump wants Saudi Arabia to join
item.description: President Trump says that the U.S.-Saudi nuclear deal is contingent on Saudi Arabia joining the Abraham Accords, which would require them to normalize diplomatic relations with Israel. CBS News' Michael George reports.

item.title: What's a good student loan interest rate for fall 2026?
item.description: Hoping for the most affordable student loan rates this fall? Here's what separates a good rate from a costly one.

item.title: Democrats eye these 2 states to kick off 2028 primary calendar
item.description: Democrats are meeting to discuss the 2028 presidential nomination schedule. Some Democrats favor South Carolina to lead the 2028 primaries, while others prefer Nevada. CBS News political contributor Joel Payne explains why state order matters.

item.title: Sen. Peters wants to know why ICE bought spyware that can take control of phones
item.description: U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement has purchased commercial spyware that can covertly take control of phones, collect messages, photos and location information.

item.title: Cyclospora outbreak linked to iceberg lettuce expands to 4 more states
item.description: The cyclosporiasis outbreak, the nation's largest, is currently affecting nine states.

item.title: Tighter security in place ahead of rescheduled White House Correspondents' Dinner
item.description: U.S. Secret Service director Sean Curran spoke about a rise in threats ahead of the rescheduled White House Correspondents' Dinner. CBS News' Nicole Sganga reports.

item.title: White House cites forced labor concerns as legal justification for new tariffs
item.description: The White House announced it's placing tariffs on 60 countries, citing forced labor concerns, after the Supreme Court ruled the Trump administration must find proper legal justification to impose them. CBS News' Nancy Cordes explains.

item.title: Here are the new tariff rates on 60 countries targeted by President Trump
item.description: President Trump announced new tariffs of up to 12.5% to replace expiring Section 122 levies. Here are the rates for each country.

item.title: Why Yemen's Houthi rebels are attacking Saudi oil tankers in Red Sea
item.description: Trade routes are under escalating pressure as troubles continue in the Strait of Hormuz and Houthi rebels attack Saudi oil tankers in the Red Sea. CBS News' Courtney Kealy has more.

item.title: NYPD says dozens rescued from human trafficking during World Cup
item.description: There were 89 people arrested in the New York area over the course of the World Cup, the NYPD said.

item.title: Ex-U.S. attorney in Seattle sues Trump over firing
item.description: Roger Rogoff was fired from his role as U.S. attorney in Seattle shortly after judges on the U.S. District Court for the Western District of Washington appointed him to the job.

item.title: NYPD arrests 89 suspected human traffickers, rescues dozens of victims during World Cup
item.description: While fans gathered to celebrate the 2026 FIFA World Cup, detectives with the New York Police Department were working to catch human traffickers. CBS News' Anna Schecter has more.

item.title: Heavy rain causes major flooding in parts of North Carolina
item.description: Parts of North Carolina were hit by heavy rain on Thursday, causing chaos on the roads. There's a threat of more flooding on Friday. Skyler Henry has the latest.

item.title: New details from original Nancy Guthrie ransom note
item.description: New details from the original ransom note for Nancy Guthrie were revealed on the podcast "Crime Junkie." Police are still looking for tips after the disappearance of Guthrie, the mother of "Today" show co-host Savannah Guthrie. CBS News' Mark Strassmann reports.

item.title: What to avoid if your CD account matures this August
item.description: Have a CD account maturity date on the calendar this August? Here are three things to avoid doing before then.

item.title: Pregnant woman hospitalized after cyclosporiasis diagnosis says she was "very afraid"
item.description: A woman says she was diagnosed with cyclosporiasis and admitted to the hospital, but doctors were unable to prescribe the only effective antibiotic because she's pregnant. Now that she's recovered, she says she still won't feed her family raw produce. The FDA is now investigating a new cyclospora outbreak, but the source hasn't been specified. Carter Evans reports.

item.title: Friend of man shot dead by Madison police says he "was executed" as protests continue
item.description: Protesters in Madison, Wisconsin, are demanding accountability after Corey Ruiz was fatally shot by police during an attempted arrest. CBS News' Jarred Hill reports.

item.title: FDA panel recommends loosening regulations for some peptides
item.description: The FDA panel is reviewing seven peptides and deciding if they should be added to a list of substances that can be safely produced by compounding pharmacies.

item.title: Secret Service warns of high threat levels before White House Correspondents' Dinner
item.description: Secret Service officials warned nationwide threats have reached an unprecedented level since January, nearly 40% higher than during the same period last year.

item.title: All 11 on seaplane that crashed, caught fire north of Seattle survive
item.description: A seaplane with 11 people on board crashed and caught fire a short time later in the Puget Sound area north of Seattle. All 11 survived, authorities said.

item.title: U.S. targets California-born leader of notorious Jalisco cartel
item.description: Juan Carlos Gonzalez, also known as "Pelon," is the stepson of "El Mencho" — real name Nemesio Oseguera — who was injured and later died in a military sweep.

item.title: The Federal Reserve meets next week. Could it raise interest rates?
item.description: The central bank is widely expected to stand pat on Wednesday, but persistent inflation concerns are reshaping expectations for the rest of 2026.

item.title: Trump fires U.S. attorney in Washington on heels of court appointment
item.description: Judges on the U.S. District Court for the Western District of Washington had appointed Roger Rogoff as U.S. attorney in Seattle. But he was fired shortly after.

item.title: Russian Soyuz rocket launches fresh crew to space station
item.description: The new crew will replace three other Soyuz fliers wrapping up a 240-day stay aboard the International Space Station.
